description_wiki: Duke University is a private research university in Durham, North Carolina, United States.

description_wiki: Laurinburg is a city in and the county seat of Scotland County, North Carolina, United States.
The Saturday Evening Post is an American magazine published six times a year.
Ladies' Home Journal was an American magazine that ran until 2016 and was last published by the Meredith Corporation.

description: American financial services and communications company
description_wiki: The Western Union Company is an American multinational financial services corporation headquartered in Denver, Colorado.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

description_wiki: Newton is a city in Catawba County, North Carolina, United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: neighborhood in Manhattan, New York City, New York, United States
description_wiki: Harlem is a neighborhood in Upper Manhattan, New York City.

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

description: revolution establishing the United States of America
description_wiki: The American Revolution (1765–1783) was a political movement in the Thirteen Colonies of Great Britain and the United States which the colonies founded.

The Seaboard Air Line Railroad (reporting mark SAL), known colloquially as the Seaboard Railroad during its time, was an American railroad that existed from April 14, 1900, until July 1, 1967, when it merged with the Atlantic Coast Line Railroad, its longtime rival, to form the Seaboard Coast Line Railroad.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Virginia, officially the Commonwealth of Virginia, is a state in the Southeastern and Mid-Atlantic regions of the United States between the Atlantic Coast and the Appalachian Mountains.

description: independent city near Roanoke, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Salem is an independent city in the Commonwealth of Virginia.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity
description_wiki: Methodism, also called the Methodist movement, is a Protestant Christian tradition whose origins, doctrines and practice derive from the life and teachings of John Wesley.
